rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY** GUARDIANSHIP OF HAYDEN ANGELO DE BARROS CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) On 04/08/2019 the court order page 2 line 6 states, "No other reimbursement for childcare are allowed without prior court approval. No court approval was sought for $5,385.00 in childcare expenses already reimbursed in this account. Examiner requested receipts for all entries. 2) On 04/08/2019 the court order page 2 line 9 states "Refund of $3,753.74 for life insurance premium paid shall be reflected in the next account." No such refund appears in the account. 3) Original statements not filed. [Prob. C. 2620(c).] 4) Has not filed mandatory judicial council form GC-400. 5) Please explain the legal basis for the guardianship paying legal expenses related to setting up the ward's inheritance estate. Please provide attorney timesheets for all legal expenses paid to ARCR. 6) Please provide legal authority for including the items listed on Schedule F Liabilities and file receipts for all entries. 7) Attorney fees do not appear to be mentioned in this account. The Court prefers to determine the amount of fees at the time an account is considered. [LRSF 14.56 B.] 8) Has not filed notice of hearing for the petition. Petitioner alleges co-guardian received notice. Only notice filed was 7/21/2021 for a Supplement to Third Account.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SU HWA WAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/22/21. (No earlier availability.) The Always Best Care invoice 3635 (not #3655) dated 10/07/19 attached to the second supplement filed on 7/16/21 shows the period of services to be 9/30/19 - 10/03/19 totaling $6,890.20, which was paid on 10/10/19 (reflected on 10/15/19 in the accounting) with check 934. Conservator now says that check 938 was for services from 9/30/19 - 10/06/19 ($6,890.20) and from 10/7/19 - 10/13/19 ($7,069.20). It appears that she paid twice for the period of services from 9/30/19 - 10/3/19. The order dated 8/1/18 allowed the balance of $9,097.54 in fees to be paid to Mr. Conrad. The second accounting showed a payment of $9,097.54 to Mr. Conrad on 8/9/18 pursuant to that order. This accounting shows another payment to him of $7,747.50 on 1/15/19 pursuant to that same order? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/13/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVINDER SINGH SIDHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/22/21. Still no complete courtesy copy of the petition has been received by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] The I&A filed on 2/4/20 showed a lump sum of $190,512.72 in cash, but Attachment 1 showed the insurance proceeds to be $175,335.17 and the two Bank of America accounts (not separately listed) as totaling $15,155.52, which total $190,490.69, not $190,512.72. The accounting begins with $190,482.16. Additionally, the Probate Referee's commission is shown as $75.00, but there is no Attachment 2 to the I&A showing assets appraised by the Probate Referee. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary, not "100%". [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Nothing has been filed to address the discrepancy in the identity of the decedent's father: no verified statement from the two men who were identified as the father in the original Petition for Letters of Administration (who signed the Disclaimer) or the purported stepfather listed on the death certificate as the decedent's father or from any of the decedent's siblings who could possibly attest to the identity of the decedent's biological father, etc. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/13/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD MARSHALL DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No proof of service of notice has been filed yet. (The Examiner is aware that the proof of service was uploaded for e-filing earlier but has not yet been filed as a result of the shortage of Court staff.) The accounting period exceeds the number of years this Court permits for a first account. [LRSF 14.35 A.] Original bank statements must be filed to verify the ending balances. [LRSF 14.35 D.] The banks are in Ohio, not California. [Probate Code Section 9700.] What were the initial estate expenses for which Attorney Connors was reimbursed $4,000 on 10/4/2018? What were the Public Administrator's expenses comprising the $4,453.91 she was paid on 3/12/2019? Costs must be itemized. The time sheets have not been fully reviewed yet; however, it is unclear why the personal representative, regardless of his profession, had to track the case, review tentative rulings and examiner notes, and listen in on hearings, etc., when he is represented by counsel, and why he should have billed as an attorney for such services. There was a request for special notice filed in this case on 8/10/18 but it was mailed to the attorney for Public Administrator. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/10/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF AALIYAH YASMIN CARRASCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The guardian receives social security benefits in the amount of $20,976 per year for the minor. The benefits were not placed in the blocked account and were mostly exhausted for support of the minor without court approval under Probate Code section 2422, except for a $10,000 reserve remaining in the unblocked account without a bond. The minor's father paid $2,050 in 2020 for support of the minor. The voluntary support payments from the father were given to the minor as her cash allowances without court approval under Probate Code section 2421. The guardian does not intend to pursue a child support order against the father. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLAUDE L HEATER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/28/2021. 1) The amended petition page 6 lines 10-12 alleges the account period is from 5/28/2020 to 03/05/2021. The Supplement shows the bank statement provided begins 04/01/2021. The letter from Wells Fargo is dated 07/12/2021. Please provide verification of cash balances at the end of the account period 03/05/2021. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 2) The 07/19/2021 new filings are not signed by Juyeon Song. A pleading must be in writing and must be signed by all persons joining in it. [CRC 7.103]. 3) In the 7/19/2021 filing called Argument Why FDIC insurance for estate funds greater than $250,000.00 is not required petitioner cites California Rule of Court 8015(b). There appears to be no such rule. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK CHRISTOPHER DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/28/2021. 1) When the petition seeks a non-pro rata distribution, it must show the computation on which the proposed distribution is based. Consents of interested beneficiaries must be filed. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)a.] 2) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)b.]. 3) Please explain the request for $450.00 for final petition filing fee when the filing fee was $435.00. 4) Please explain the legal basis for over $250,00.00 being kept in what appears to be a single account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER CARDENAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/28/2021. 1) The amended petition alleges "a detailed schedule of receipts and gains during administration is attached hereto at Attachment A and incorporated herein." There is no such attachment. 2) Notice of the petition was given prior to the petition being filed. [See DE-120 page 1 #1 and LRSF 14.14 A.] 3) The petition page 4 line 19 alleges the total value of the estate is $1,290,434.90. Please explain this number and calculation of statutory fees as it appears the actual number is $1,287,587.02 + $2,841.96 = $1,290,428.98. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF REYNOLD DUBBERKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/7/21. Issues necessitating continuance: No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A.] No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. The petition caption is incomplete if the petitioner is seeking full IAEA authority. Item 3f(2) of the petition is incomplete; the will was self-proving. Item 3h of the petition was not answered. Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. The Duties and Liabilities has not yet been filed. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. In addition to the missing mandatory Judicial Council forms noted above, the issues with the petition must be addressed in a supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper, and signed and verified by the proposed executor. Courtesy copies of all papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/27/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIGUEL Q URIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/21/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Item 3a of the petition is incomplete. Item 3h of the petition shows an address that differs from the one on the Duties and Liabilities. Item 8 of the petition does not show the date of death of the decedent's mother (but her address?!). [LRSF 14.18 D.] It also does not show Elena Uria's relationship to the decedent and is missing the petitioner, who is the person named in item 2. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/10/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. The Examiner is aware of the Response filed on 7/22/21 and assumes that a Petition for Probate will be filed, although the copy of the will attached to the Response is not executed or witnessed. If the objector files her own competing petition, this petition will be continued to be heard together with the later petition.

It is also noted that the petition appears to contain many errors. Petitioner is highly advised to seek legal counsel. All required documents must be filed and provided to Room 202 at least two weeks prior to the continued hearing date. A proposed order must also be provided.
